item.title: Kicking It at Hermann Park
item.description:
Friday, June 12, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: The Commons

This summer, Hermann Park Conservancy will welcome the world to Houston with Kicking It at Hermann Park, presented by ExxonMobil—a free, public celebration of culture, community, and the unifying spirit of sport. Featuring a dynamic mix of programming, diverse food offerings, live entertainment, and family-friendly activities, everything will take place in the Commons, the vibrant 26-acre play and gathering space in the southwest corner of the Park. 📍 The Commons at Hermann Park 📅 Weekend and select weekdays from June 12 – July 5 🎟️ Free & open to the public Kicking It at Hermann Park will run on weekends and select weekdays from Friday, June 12, through Sunday, July 5, offering 15 days of engaging experiences for Houstonians and visitors alike during a globally significant moment for the city. The roster of dynamic programming will include: 🎶 Live music & entertainment 🍴 Local food trucks & culinary offerings 🎬 Outdoor movie nights ⚽ Soccer-inspired play & interactive experiences 🌿 Nature-based activities 👨‍👩‍👧 Family-friendly programming 🎭 Cultural celebrations highlighting Houston’s diversity Thank you to ExxonMobil for their generous support! +++ DATES & THEMES Stay tuned for more details and a complete programming calendar. Activities are subject to change and more may be added. Visit the Conservancy tent on-site in the Commons for any questions! WEEKEND ONE (JUNE 12 – JUNE 14) PDF SCHEDULE HERE. Friday, June 12 (5 – 9 p.m.): Kickoff in the Park 5 – 6 PM: Trees for Texas: A Backyard Tree Walk (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 5 – 7 PM: Carousel Karaoke (Lott Family Carousel – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: Seeds for Success: The Art of Starting a Garden (Cullen Pavilion – The Commons) 7 – 8 PM: Perennials in the Park: Garden Discovery Tour (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 7 – 9 PM: Open Decks: Live DJ Experience (Powell Foundation Terrace – between Water Play Area and Space Adventure in the Commons) Saturday, June 13 (3 – 10 p.m.): Texas Heritage 3 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 3 – 4 PM: T is for Texas! Twanda’s Theatre on Wheels from Young Audiences of Houston (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 3 – 4 PM: Hidden Invaders: Understanding Invasive Species (Next to Lott Family Carousel – The Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Rhythms of Tradition: Mixteco Ballet Folklorico from Young Audiences of Houston (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Winding Paths of the Japanese Garden: Guided Tour (Japanese Garden) 5 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 5 -6 PM: Perennials in the Park Tour (McGovern Centennial Gardens) 5 – 8 PM: Hooked on Fishing! Youth Fishing with 4-H (Fishing Pier – The Commons) 5 – 8 PM: DJ Areal: Music in the Commons (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 8:30 PM: Moonlight Movie Nights presented by Goya: The Parent Trap (Doreen Stoller Lawn – The Commons) Sunday, June 14 (3 – 9 p.m.): Family & Global Community Day 3 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 4 – 5 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 5 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 5 – 6 PM: Mosaic Workshop from Young Audiences of Houston (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 5 – 6 PM: Bats-a-Billion: Discover the World of Bats (Cullen Pavilion – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: Winding Paths of the Japanese Garden: Guided Tour (Japanese Garden) 7 – 9 PM: Jazz in the Park with Bayou City Jazz Band (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) Wednesday, June 17 (3 – 9 p.m.): Wellness in the Park – THIS DATE HAS BEEN CANCELED DUE TO INCLEMENT WEATHER! Friday, June 19 (5 – 9 p.m.): Juneteenth Celebration Saturday, June 20 (3 – 10 p.m.): Global Rhythms Sunday, June 21 (3 – 9 p.m.): Community & Wellness Day Tuesday, June 23 (3 – 9 p.m.): Youth + Play + Sustainability Day Friday, June 26 (5 – 9 p.m.): Latin Night Saturday, June 27 (3 – 10 p.m.): Global DJ Night Sunday, June 28 (3 – 9 p.m.): International Culture Day Monday, June 29 (3 – 9 p.m.): Arts + Nature Day Friday, July 3 (5 – 10 p.m.): Summer Nights in the Park Saturday, July 4 (3 – 7 p.m.): Celebrate America – Houston Style Sunday, July 5 (3 – 9 p.m.): Closing Celebration +++ MOONLIGHT MOVIE NIGHTS Presented by Goya As part of the summer offerings, enjoy Moonlight Movie Nights, presented by Goya, in the Commons! Movies will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. These movies are free and open to the public. Saturday, June 13: The Parent Trap (1998; PG) Saturday, June 20: A Bug’s Life (1998; G) Saturday, June 27: Lilo & Stitch (2002; PG) Friday, July 3: National Treasure (2004; PG) Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. +++ To inquire about sponsorship opportunities and ways to get involved, email community@hermannpark.org.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.description:
Tuesday, June 16, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Join us for a cross-generational literacy experience! Family Storytime features a story followed by a craft that accompanies the reading. All craft supplies are included as a part of this free program.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park (CANCELED DUE TO WEATHER)
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

THIS EVENT HAS BEEN CANCELED DUE TO THE WEATHER. Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Buffalo Bayou Cruise
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: ALLEN’S LANDING, 1001 Commerce Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

These 45-minute boat rides are a great way to spend time with your family and friends. Escape the bustle of the city and enjoy the cool breeze as you glide along Buffalo Bayou, through the heart of downtown Houston. Look for graceful herons, turtles, fish, and even an occasional alligator on the banks. Tours leave promptly on the hour beginning at 5 PM.   Cost Adults: $20 Children: $16 (ages 4-12)   Departure Location Allen’s Landing (1019 Commerce St.) Weather permitting. Reservations Required. Children under the age of 4 are not permitted on the pontoon boat.   PURCHASE TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

item.title: Buffalo Bayou Park Wellness Walk
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: THE WATER WORKS, 105 Sabine Street, Houston, TX, 77007, United States

Join Buffalo Bayou Partnership on a free Wellness Walk led by Laura Conely, founder of Urban Paths. Anyone and everyone is invited to walk through the paths of Buffalo Bayou Park while discussing topics including mental and physical health as well as the benefits of being exposed to nature. En Español El primer miércoles de cada mes, la traductora Marcella Cabrera se unirá al grupo para ofrecer la caminata guiada en español. Cost Free Duration 90 minutes / 3 miles Meeting Location The Water Works in Buffalo Bayou Park (105-B Sabine St) FREE REGISTRATION

item.title: Japanese Garden Volunteer Day
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Japanese Garden, Hermann Park Japanese Garden, Houston, TX, 77035, United States

Join us every Thursday for hands-on gardening projects at the Japanese Garden. From 9 a.m. to 12 p.m., come help with seasonal tasks like planting, weeding, mulching, and pruning. A great way to connect with others and make a visible impact in the Park! No experience needed. All ages are welcome. Click here to sign up!

Event details

item.title: Summer Camp All Grown Up
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Why should kids have all the fun? Treat yourself to all the joys of summer camp, but for grown-ups! Unwind after a stressful day with hands-on nature activities, crafts, and outdoor exploration, PLUS a simple snack and a refreshing beer from Saint Arnold Brewing Company. Please dress for the weather and wear walking shoes. $22 member / $37 nonmember per classThursday, June 18 and July 16Ages 21+ Micro World Thursday, June 18 There’s a whole world of activity going on under our noses. Using hand lenses and microscopes, we’ll hunt for the tiny creatures that make our ecosystems function, from roly-polies to plankton. We’ll even create a miniature world to take home: a micro-terrarium! register online CSI Nature Thursday, July 16 Nature is full of mysteries, and you can find clues everywhere if you know what to look for. Learn about signs of wildlife activity like tracks and scat, make your own gourmet chocolate “scat snacks,” and search the trails for evidence of the Arboretum’s “usual suspects.” register online

From Houston Arboretum & Nature Center

Event details

item.title: Buffalo Bayou Cruise: Bats
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 7:20 PM

Location: ALLEN’S LANDING, 1001 Commerce Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Sail the scenic waterway on a 90-minute, guided tour from downtown to Waugh Drive Bridge and back to watch the emergence of the 250,000+ Mexican free-tailed bats that live under the bridge. Our knowledgeable guide will share details about the bats’ eating patterns, behavior and contributions to the bayou’s ecosystem. Space is limited, and boat tours fill up fast. Boat departs 45 minutes before sunset. Cost Starting at $30/person Duration 90 minutes Departure location Allen’s Landing (1019 Commerce St.) Please note, children must be at least four years old to board the pontoon boat.   PURCHASE TICKETS

Event details

item.title: Bat Chat
item.description:
Friday, June 19, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Waugh Drive Bat Bridge, 1

Stroll to Waugh Drive Bridge for a Bat Chat with the Houston Area Bat Team and see the bat colony up close! Did you know? These population of Mexican Free-tailed Bats at the Waugh Bridge can reach a peak of approximately 300,000 in the summer months! Around sunset, the colony leaves the crevices beneath the bridge and create a whirlwind-like formation as they emerge. Join the Houston Area Bat Team to learn and discover as they share fun, educational facts and insights about these miniature, flying mammals! Invite your family and friends, bring a picnic blanket, wear your bug spray, and admire this unique phenomena. Bat Chats are held every Friday evening from May to August as part of our Summer Species series, starting 30 minutes before sunset.   Cost Free Time 7:30 – 8:30pm (30 minutes before sunset) Meeting Location Waugh Bridge Bat Colony Observation Deck (along Allen Parkway) REGISTRATION

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Sound Healing Meditation
item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: BUFFALO BAYOU PARK CISTERN, 105 Sabine Street, Houston, TX, 77007, United States

Step into a deeper stillness inside the spacious and tranquil Buffalo Bayou Park Cistern. Offered in collaboration with UNIONisCREATION™, this meditation and sound experience invites guests into a space of resonance, restoration, and inner alignment. Through gentle vibrations of singing bowls, wind chimes, and a gong, along with guided awareness, participants are supported in reconnecting body and mind. This series is more than meditation – it is a practice of listening, feeling, and returning to center within one of Houston’s most iconic, immersive spaces. Each session unfolds as a shared moment of reflection, connection, and sound-guided stillness. Guests should bring their own cushions or mats. No food or drinks are allowed inside the Cistern. Cost: $20 PURCHASE TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Buffalo Bayou Cruise: History
item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: ALLEN’S LANDING, 1001 Commerce Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Look back at Houston’s history while cruising Buffalo Bayou on a 90-minute tour with a local historian. Your guide will share stories of the Allen brothers and provide historical information about the people, places, and events along the bayou that helped shape Houston.   Cost Adults: $40 Children: $32 (ages 4-12)   Departure Location Allen’s Landing (1019 Commerce St.) Weather permitting. Reservations Required. Children under the age of 4 are not permitted on the pontoon boat.   PURCHASE TICKETS

item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 5 PM

$37 member / $52 nonmemberAges 21+ Celebrate the longest day of the year by cultivating your creativity! Enjoy Saint Arnold Brewing Company beer while you make crafts and décor using natural materials. We’ll make seasonal wreaths, ornaments, beeswax candles, and more. Please dress for the weather and wear clothes you don’t mind getting messy. Register Online

item.title: Bikes & Bats
item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: THE WATER WORKS, 105 Sabine Street, Houston, TX, 77007, United States

Join Buffalo Bayou Partnership and BikeHouston for a batty good time with a bike ride to the Waugh Bridge Bat Colony! Bring your bike for an evening ride along Buffalo Bayou to the Waugh Drive Bridge, where you’ll witness an unforgettable sight—more than 300,000 Mexican free-tailed bats emerging at sunset. Along the way, bat expert DeAndra Ramsey will share insights about these fascinating and important creatures—affectionately known as “sky puppies.” Spots are limited, so reserve yours today! Cost $10 Duration 2 hours / 3 miles Meeting Location The Water Works in Buffalo Bayou Park (105-B Sabine St) TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

item.title: Wellness Walk
item.description:
Tuesday, June 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Join Laura Conely of Urban Paths for a fun evening as we explore the trails and beauty of Hermann Park on a guided 3-mile experience. All are invited to this conversationally paced walk; friendly pups on leash are welcome too! This event is free, but registration is required and space is limited. Sign up HERE. Participants will meet outside the McGovern Centennial Gardens, located at 1500 Hermann Drive, with parking available in adjacent Lot C. Laura Conely is the founder of Urban Paths, a walking wellness company. As a certified teacher and coach, she leads guided Wellness Walks that bring individuals of all ages and fitness levels together to experience the profound health benefits of: Nature, Art, Movement, and Connection.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

En Español El primer miércoles de cada mes, la traductora Marcella Cabrera se unirá al grupo para ofrecer la caminata guiada en español. item.description:
Thursday, June 25, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: ALLEN’S LANDING, 1001 Commerce Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

These 45-minute boat rides are a great way to spend time with your family and friends. Escape the bustle of the city and enjoy the cool breeze as you glide along Buffalo Bayou, through the heart of downtown Houston. Look for graceful herons, turtles, fish, and even an occasional alligator on the banks. Tours leave promptly on the hour beginning at 5 PM.   Cost Adults: $20 Children: $16 (ages 4-12)   Departure Location Allen’s Landing (1019 Commerce St.) Weather permitting. Reservations Required. Event details

item.title: Buffalo Bayou Cruise: Bats
item.description:
Friday, June 26, 2026 at 7:20 PM

Location: ALLEN’S LANDING, 1001 Commerce Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Sail the scenic waterway on a 90-minute, guided tour from downtown to Waugh Drive Bridge and back to watch the emergence of the 250,000+ Mexican free-tailed bats that live under the bridge. Our knowledgeable guide will share details about the bats’ eating patterns, behavior and contributions to the bayou’s ecosystem. Space is limited, and boat tours fill up fast. Boat departs 45 minutes before sunset. Cost Starting at $30/person Duration 90 minutes Departure location Allen’s Landing (1019 Commerce St.) Please note, children must be at least four years old to board the pontoon boat.   PURCHASE TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Bat Chat
item.description:
Friday, June 26, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Waugh Drive Bat Bridge, 1

Stroll to Waugh Drive Bridge for a Bat Chat with the Houston Area Bat Team and see the bat colony up close! Did you know? These population of Mexican Free-tailed Bats at the Waugh Bridge can reach a peak of approximately 300,000 in the summer months! Around sunset, the colony leaves the crevices beneath the bridge and create a whirlwind-like formation as they emerge. Join the Houston Area Bat Team to learn and discover as they share fun, educational facts and insights about these miniature, flying mammals! Invite your family and friends, bring a picnic blanket, wear your bug spray, and admire this unique phenomena. Bat Chats are held every Friday evening from May to August as part of our Summer Species series, starting 30 minutes before sunset.   Cost Free Time 7:30 – 8:30pm (30 minutes before sunset) Meeting Location Waugh Bridge Bat Colony Observation Deck (along Allen Parkway) REGISTRATION

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Fourth Saturday Phenology Walk
item.description:
Saturday, June 27, 2026 at 9 AM

Fourth Saturday of the Month9:00 – 11:00 amFree, donations appreciatedAll ages, minors must be accompanied by an adult Meet in front of the Nature Center building Discover the hidden seasonal rhythms of nature firsthand on our guided Phenology Walks, where we track the life cycles of different plants across ecosystems, from the first spring bud burst to winter dormancy. You’ll learn how shifting weather patterns and climate change influence the timing of these important events in our local ecosystems and how you can become a community scientist, contributing vital data from your own backyard to a global research initiative. These relaxing morning walks are led by scientists from Rice University and staff from the Arboretum. We welcome nature enthusiasts of all ages, however kids 12 years and younger must be accompanied by a guardian. Learn more about the study of phenology and ways you can contribute at https://www.usanpn.org/nn Community Science Partner

From Houston Arboretum & Nature Center

Event details

item.title: Sound Healing Meditation
item.description:
Saturday, June 27, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: BUFFALO BAYOU PARK CISTERN, 105 Sabine Street, Houston, TX, 77007, United States

Step into a deeper stillness inside the spacious and tranquil Buffalo Bayou Park Cistern. Offered in collaboration with UNIONisCREATION™, this meditation and sound experience invites guests into a space of resonance, restoration, and inner alignment. Through gentle vibrations of singing bowls, wind chimes, and a gong, along with guided awareness, participants are supported in reconnecting body and mind. This series is more than meditation – it is a practice of listening, feeling, and returning to center within one of Houston’s most iconic, immersive spaces. Each session unfolds as a shared moment of reflection, connection, and sound-guided stillness. Guests should bring their own cushions or mats. No food or drinks are allowed inside the Cistern. Cost: $20 PURCHASE TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Gardening for Bees
item.description:
Saturday, June 27, 2026 at 10 AM

$22 member / $37 nonmemberAges 12+, minors must be accompanied by an adult Set the table for bees in your garden! Learn all about the best native plants for bees, techniques to help bees survive the extremes of summer and winter, and how to provide nesting space for these critical pollinators. Please dress for the weather and wear walking shoes. register online

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Red, White & Cruise
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: Lake Plaza

Join us for Red, White & Cruise where you can watch Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show light up the sky from the peaceful waters of Hermann Park, with a special pedal boat rental from 8:30–10:30 p.m. on Saturday, July 4! 🎇 As we celebrate 250 years of American independence, gather your crew and watch the sky come alive with fireworks while cruising across the lake in your own pedal boat under the stars. 🌟  Saturday, July 4 8:30 – 10:30 p.m.  🎟️ $150 per boat (up to 4 people) includes: 🕘 Boat rental up to 2 hours 🍿 Drinks & snacks 🎊 Festive accessories 🅿️ Reserved parking in Lot I near Lake Plaza ⛵ Board anytime between 8:30–9:30 p.m. and enjoy the show from the water (fireworks begin at approximately 9:45 p.m., boats must return once fireworks finish) BOOK YOUR BOAT HERE!  🚨 Important Info: One rider per boat must be 18+ All riders 18+ must sign a digital waiver before boarding At least one rider must be 18+, with a physical government ID, and sign a waiver before boarding Tickets are non-refundable Event is rain or shine per Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan In the event of lightning, pedal boats will close, and refunds will be issued  The purchase of a ticket includes an up-to 2-hour pedal boat ride for up to 4 people to enjoy the sight of Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show, drinks, snacks, festive treats, and a reserved parking spot in Lot I near Lott Hall by Lake Plaza (starting at 8 p.m.). Only one ticket purchase of $150 is needed for a 4-person boat (i.e. it is the cost per boat, not per rider).  A reminder that at least one rider per boat must be 18 years or older.  All riders 18 years or older must sign a digital waiver before boarding. Tickets are non-refundable. We are following Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan for their event and fireworks show which is rain or shine. If there is lightning in the area, we will close the pedal boats and ticket holders will receive a refund.  Please arrive early to allow time to park in Lot I as roads in the Park will be closed by HPD and closures are subject to change based on their purview.  

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Buffalo Bayou Park Wellness Walk
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: THE WATER WORKS, 105 Sabine Street, Houston, TX, 77007, United States

Join Buffalo Bayou Partnership on a free Wellness Walk led by Laura Conely, founder of Urban Paths. Anyone and everyone is invited to walk through the paths of Buffalo Bayou Park while discussing topics including mental and physical health as well as the benefits of being exposed to nature. En Español El primer miércoles de cada mes, la traductora Marcella Cabrera se unirá al grupo para ofrecer la caminata guiada en español. Cost Free Duration 90 minutes / 3 miles Meeting Location The Water Works in Buffalo Bayou Park (105-B Sabine St) FREE REGISTRATION

Event details

item.title: Buffalo Bayou Cruise: Bats
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 7:20 PM

Location: ALLEN’S LANDING, 1001 Commerce Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Sail the scenic waterway on a 90-minute, guided tour from downtown to Waugh Drive Bridge and back to watch the emergence of the 250,000+ Mexican free-tailed bats that live under the bridge. Our knowledgeable guide will share details about the bats’ eating patterns, behavior and contributions to the bayou’s ecosystem. Space is limited, and boat tours fill up fast. Boat departs 45 minutes before sunset. Cost Starting at $30/person Duration 90 minutes Departure location Allen’s Landing (1019 Commerce St.) Please note, children must be at least four years old to board the pontoon boat.   PURCHASE TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Bat Chat
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Waugh Drive Bat Bridge, 1

Stroll to Waugh Drive Bridge for a Bat Chat with the Houston Area Bat Team and see the bat colony up close! Did you know? These population of Mexican Free-tailed Bats at the Waugh Bridge can reach a peak of approximately 300,000 in the summer months! Around sunset, the colony leaves the crevices beneath the bridge and create a whirlwind-like formation as they emerge. Join the Houston Area Bat Team to learn and discover as they share fun, educational facts and insights about these miniature, flying mammals! Invite your family and friends, bring a picnic blanket, wear your bug spray, and admire this unique phenomena. Bat Chats are held every Friday evening from May to August as part of our Summer Species series, starting 30 minutes before sunset.   Cost Free Time 7:30 – 8:30pm (30 minutes before sunset) Meeting Location Waugh Bridge Bat Colony Observation Deck (along Allen Parkway) FREE REGISTRATION

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Second Saturday Bird Survey
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 8 AM

2nd Saturday of the month8:00 – 10:00 amFree, but donations appreciatedAll ages, minors must be accompanied by an adult Meet in front of the Nature Center building Work on your bird watching skills while helping the Arboretum monitor bird populations on a fun, relaxed morning walk. Join us on the second Saturday of each month in the courtyard behind the building for a two-hour bird survey led by Arboretum staff members, Kelsey Low and Theo Ostler. We welcome all levels of bird watching skill and all ages (as long as you’re quiet) – but no dogs, please! If you have binoculars of your own, please bring them. We have a limited number of binoculars available to borrow. The June 13, 2026 Bird Survey has been cancelled. Please join us in July!

From Houston Arboretum & Nature Center

Event details

item.title: Sound Healing Meditation
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: BUFFALO BAYOU PARK CISTERN, 105 Sabine Street, Houston, TX, 77007, United States

Step into a deeper stillness inside the spacious and tranquil Buffalo Bayou Park Cistern. Offered in collaboration with UNIONisCREATION™, this meditation and sound experience invites guests into a space of resonance, restoration, and inner alignment. Through gentle vibrations of singing bowls, wind chimes, and a gong, along with guided awareness, participants are supported in reconnecting body and mind. This series is more than meditation – it is a practice of listening, feeling, and returning to center within one of Houston’s most iconic, immersive spaces. Each session unfolds as a shared moment of reflection, connection, and sound-guided stillness. Guests should bring their own cushions or mats. No food or drinks are allowed inside the Cistern. Cost: $20 PURCHASE TICKETS

From Buffalo Bayou Partnership

Event details

item.title: Blood Drive
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 10 AM

The Houston Arboretum is partnering with the Gulf Coast Regional Blood Center to host regular blood drives at the Arboretum. Click a date below to sign up to donate blood. All types of blood are needed to support the needs of our city. All successful donors will receive a voucher for a free Arboretum Individual Membership! 2026 january 3 february 7 March 7 April 4 may 2 june 13 july 11 august 1 september 26 october 3 november 7 december 12 If all time slots are grayed out, there are no more appointments available for that day. If you are coming to donate, please use the 610 Entrance for easiest access to the Donor Coach. Questions? Donor AdvocatesDo you have questions about your eligibility to give blood? Need more information about a test result? Please contact us with any medical questions you may have before, during or after your donation.  Donor Advocates team is available:  8 a.m. to 4 p.m. Monday–Friday  Email: donoradvocacy@giveblood.org | Call: (713) 791-6608 The Blood Center will respond to your email within 24 hours during regular business hours. You may also check the Eligibility Page for basic eligibility questions: https://www.giveblood.org/donate/check-eligibility/ Customer ExperienceDo you need to change your appointment? Have a question about our Donor Rewards program? Can’t find your Donor ID? Customer Experience team is available:  8 a.m. to 4 p.m. Monday–FridayEmail: cs@giveblood.org | Call: (713) 791-6373 or +1 (877) 948-5433 toll-freeThe Blood Center will respond to your email within 24 hours during regular business hours. You may also email Alyssa Giraldo at agiraldo@giveblood.org to reschedule your appointment.

From Houston Arboretum & Nature Center

Event details

item.title: Summer Camp All Grown Up
item.description:
Thursday, July 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Why should kids have all the fun? Treat yourself to all the joys of summer camp, but for grown-ups! Unwind after a stressful day with hands-on nature activities, crafts, and outdoor exploration, PLUS a simple snack and a refreshing beer from Saint Arnold Brewing Company. Please dress for the weather and wear walking shoes. $22 member / $37 nonmember per classThursday, June 18 and July 16Ages 21+ Micro World Thursday, June 18 There’s a whole world of activity going on under our noses. Using hand lenses and microscopes, we’ll hunt for the tiny creatures that make our ecosystems function, from roly-polies to plankton. We’ll even create a miniature world to take home: a micro-terrarium! register online CSI Nature Thursday, July 16 Nature is full of mysteries, and you can find clues everywhere if you know what to look for. Learn about signs of wildlife activity like tracks and scat, make your own gourmet chocolate “scat snacks,” and search the trails for evidence of the Arboretum’s “usual suspects.” register online

From Houston Arboretum & Nature Center

Event details

item.title: Opossums & Popsicles
item.description:
Saturday, July 18, 2026 at 6:30 PM

$37 member / $52 nonmember / $22 children ages 5-12Instructor: Wildlife Center of TexasAges 5+ Chill out with some of the coolest critters around! Grab a frozen treat, enjoy a refreshing root beer (or regular beer) from Saint Arnold Brewing Company, and get up close and personal with live wild animals from Wildlife Center of Texas. You’ll learn all about Houston’s amazing urban wildlife – including opossums – and then you’ll go on a guided hike to enjoy a truly wild night out. Please dress for the weather and wear walking shoes Register online Instructor Bio: The Wildlife Center of Texas is one of the largest rehabilitation centers in the country, admitting nearly 10,000 wild patients each year. Its mission is to care for all injured, orphaned, ill, and oiled wildlife through wildlife rehabilitation, public education, and oiled wildlife response.
